differentials.name: Tension pneumothorax (clinical diagnosis) =  accumulation of air within pleural space ← free ingress + limited egress of air
differentials.raw: Tension pneumothorax (clinical diagnosis) =  accumulation of air within pleural space ← free ingress + limited egress of air

differentials.name: Subpulmonic / anterolateral pneumothorax
differentials.raw: Subpulmonic / anterolateral pneumothorax (2nd most common location)

differentials.name: Posteromedial pneumothorax
differentials.raw: Posteromedial pneumothorax (in presence of lower lobe collapse)

differentials.name: Pneumothorax → outlines pulmonary ligament Prognosis:  resorption of pneumothorax occurs at a rate of 1.25% per day
differentials.raw: Pneumothorax → outlines pulmonary ligament Prognosis:  resorption of pneumothorax occurs at a rate of 1.25% per day (accelerated by increasing inspired oxygen concentrations)

context: Pneumothorax =  accumulation of air within pleural space Pathophysiology:   disruption of visceral pleura / trauma to parietal pleura Cause: Types: Pathophysiology: intrapleural pressure exceeds atmospheric pressure in lung during expiration (check-valve mechanism) Frequency:   in 3-5% of patients with spontaneous pneumothorax, higher in barotrauma √  hyperexpanded ipsilateral chest √  mediastinal shift to contralateral side √  contralateral displacement of anterior junction line √  "deep sulcus" sign = on frontal view larger lateral costodiaphragmatic recess than on opposite side √  flattenin
